revision.comment: 子守神社一般の説明に。幕張の子守神社の系統がわからない
revision.text:
'''子守神社'''（こもりじんじゃ、こまもりじんじゃ）という名前の[[神社]]は、日本各地に存在する。名前の通り、[[子供]]の守護神としての「子守神」を祀る神社であり、[[氏神]]とされたり、氏神神社の境内に末社として祀られたりする。'''籠守'''、'''子護'''などとも書かれる。
子守神社には2つの系統がある。1つは[[水分神]]（みくまりのかみ）の系統、もう1つは熊野本宮大社第八殿・子守宮の系統である。
水分神は本来は水の配分を司どる[[水神]]であるが、「みくまり」が訛って「みこもり（御子守）」となり、子守神ともみなされるようになった。総本社は[[吉野水分神社]]（[[奈良県]][[吉野町 (奈良県)|吉野町]]）である。この系統の著名な神社には以下のようなものがある。 *[[子守神社 (可児市)|子守神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]） *[[子守神社 (京丹波市)|子守神社]]（[[京都府]][[京丹波町]]） *[[籠守勝手神社]]（[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]） *[[子守勝手神社]]（京都府[[長岡京市]]）
熊野本宮大社第八殿・子守宮は『[[梁塵秘抄]]』に「神の家の子公達（きうだち）は、八幡の若宮、熊野の若王子・子守御前」と詠まれた熊野権現の[[御子神]]である。この系統の神社には、[[藤白神社]]（和歌山県海南市）末社の楠神社（通称 子守社）などがある。
他に、以下のものがある。 *[[子守神社 (千葉市)]]（[[千葉県]][[千葉市]]）
[[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|こもりしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: lk
revision.text:
'''子守神社'''（こもりじんじゃ、こまもりじんじゃ）という名前の[[神社]]は、日本各地に存在する。名前の通り、[[子供]]の守護神としての「子守神」を祀る神社であり、[[氏神]]とされたり、氏神神社の境内に末社として祀られたりする。'''籠守'''、'''子護'''などとも書かれる。
子守神社には2つの系統がある。1つは[[水分神]]（みくまりのかみ）の系統、もう1つは熊野本宮大社第八殿・子守宮の系統である。
水分神は本来は水の配分を司どる[[水神]]であるが、「みくまり」が訛って「みこもり（御子守）」となり、子守神ともみなされるようになった。総本社は[[吉野水分神社]]（[[奈良県]][[吉野町]]）である。この系統の著名な神社には以下のようなものがある。 *[[子守神社 (可児市)|子守神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]） *[[子守神社 (京丹波市)|子守神社]]（[[京都府]][[京丹波町]]） *[[籠守勝手神社]]（[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]） *[[子守勝手神社]]（京都府[[長岡京市]]）
熊野本宮大社第八殿・子守宮は『[[梁塵秘抄]]』に「神の家の子公達（きうだち）は、八幡の若宮、熊野の若王子・子守御前」と詠まれた熊野権現の[[御子神]]である。この系統の神社には、[[藤白神社]]（和歌山県海南市）末社の楠神社（通称 子守社）などがある。
他に、以下のものがある。 *[[子守神社 (千葉市)]]（[[千葉県]][[千葉市]]）
{{DEFAULTSORT:こもりしんしや}} [[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)]] {{shinto-stub}}

水分神は本来は水の配分を司どる[[水神]]であるが、「みくまり」が訛って「みこもり（御子守）」となり、子守神ともみなされるようになった。総本社は[[吉野水分神社]]（[[奈良県]][[吉野町]]）である。

同名の神社として以下のものがある。 == 水分神系 == *[[子守神社 (可児市)|子守神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]） *[[子守神社 (京丹波市)|子守神社]]（[[京都府]][[京丹波町]]） *[[籠守勝手神社]]（[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]） *[[子守勝手神社]]（京都府[[長岡京市]]） == その他 == * [[子守神社 (千葉市)]]（[[千葉県]][[千葉市]]） * [[巨椋神社]]（[[巨椋神社#子守神社|子守神社 (宇治市)]]）（[[京都府]][[宇治市]]）
== 関連項目 == * [[子安神社]] * [[子安地蔵]] ** [[子安地蔵堂]] ** [[子安地蔵寺]] * [[子守地蔵]] * [[子育地蔵]] * [[水天宮]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護. Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the Mikumar..."
revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Topics == * [[Koasu Shrine]] * [[Koyasu Jizō]] * [[Koyasu Jizō Hall]] * [[Koyasu Jizō Temple]] * [[Komori Jizō]] * [[Kosodate Jizō]] * [[Suitengū Shrine]]

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Topics == * [[Koasu Shrine]] * [[Koyasu Jizō]] * [[Koyasu Jizō Hall]] * [[Koyasu Jizō Temple]] * [[Komori Jizō]] * [[Kosodate Jizō]] * [[Suitengū Shrine]]

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Topics == * [[Koasu Shrine]] * [[Koyasu Jizō]] ''' [[Koyasu Jizō Hall]] ''' [[Koyasu Jizō Temple]] * [[Komori Jizō]] * [[Kosodate Jizō]] * [[Suitengū Shrine]]

'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].

The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

revision.comment: running process on images category
revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

revision.comment: /* その他 */
revision.text:
'''子守神社'''（こもりじんじゃ、こまもりじんじゃ）は、日本各地に存在する[[神社]]である。名前の通り、[[子供]]の守護神としての「子守神」を祀る神社であり、[[氏神]]とされたり、氏神神社の境内に末社として祀られている。'''籠守'''、'''子護'''などとも書かれる。
子守神社には2つの系統がある。1つは[[水分神]]（みくまりのかみ）の系統、もう1つは[[熊野本宮大社]]第八殿・子守宮の系統である。
水分神は本来は水の配分を司どる[[水神]]であるが、「みくまり」が訛って「みこもり（御子守）」となり、子守神ともみなされるようになった。総本社は[[吉野水分神社]]（[[奈良県]][[吉野町]]）である。
熊野本宮大社第八殿・子守宮は『[[梁塵秘抄]]』に「神の家の子公達（きうだち）は、八幡の若宮、熊野の若王子・子守御前」と詠まれた熊野権現の[[御子神]]である。この系統の神社には、[[藤白神社]]（和歌山県海南市）末社の楠神社（通称 子守社）などがある。
他にも、[[率川神社]]は子守明神と呼ばれている。
同名の神社として以下のものがある。 == 水分神系 == *[http://kamakurasi.air-nifty.com/blog/2024/05/post-70ad8e.html 子守神社] ([[神奈川県鎌倉市]]) *[[子守神社 (可児市)|子守神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]） *[[子守神社 (京丹波市)|子守神社]]（[[京都府]][[京丹波町]]） *[[籠守勝手神社]]（[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]） *[[子守勝手神社]]（京都府[[長岡京市]]） == その他 == * [[子守神社 (千葉市)]]（[[千葉県]][[千葉市]]） * [[子守神社 (河津町大鍋)]]（ねのかみじんじゃ、[[静岡県]][[河津町]]大鍋） * [[巨椋神社#子守神社|子守神社 (宇治市)]]（[[巨椋神社]]境内社）（[[京都府]][[宇治市]]） * [[率川神社]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]） * [[子守神社(平群町)|子守神社]]（[[奈良県]][[平群町]]）
== 関連項目 == * [[子安神社]] * [[子安地蔵]] ** [[子安地蔵堂]] ** [[子安地蔵寺]] * [[子守地蔵]] * [[子育地蔵]] * [[水天宮]]

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Aqqitional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Aqqitional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
A[[Category:11 to dd]]dditional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "category:" to "Category:"
revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]]. [[ja:子守神社]]
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社|12=draft}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄|12=draft}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=draft}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=draft}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=draft}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=draft}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|12=draft}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|12=draft}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=draft}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|12=draft}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|12=draft}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|12=draft}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|12=draft}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=draft}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|12=draft}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮|12=draft}}

Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|ja|吉野水分神社|12=draft}} in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|ja|梁塵秘抄|12=draft}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=draft}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=draft}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=draft}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=draft}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|12=draft}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|12=draft}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=draft}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|12=draft}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|12=draft}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|12=draft}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|12=draft}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=draft}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|12=draft}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''子守神社'''（こもりじんじゃ、こまもりじんじゃ）は、日本各地に存在する[[神社]]である。名前の通り、[[子供]]の守護神としての「子守神」を祀る神社であり、[[氏神]]とされたり、氏神神社の境内に末社として祀られている。'''籠守'''、'''子護'''などとも書かれる。
子守神社には2つの系統がある。1つは[[水分神]]（みくまりのかみ）の系統、もう1つは[[熊野本宮大社]]第八殿・子守宮の系統である。
水分神は本来は水の配分を司どる[[水神]]であるが、「みくまり」が訛って「みこもり（御子守）」となり、子守神ともみなされるようになった。総本社は[[吉野水分神社]]（[[奈良県]][[吉野町]]）である。
熊野本宮大社第八殿・子守宮は『[[梁塵秘抄]]』に「神の家の子公達（きうだち）は、八幡の若宮、熊野の若王子・子守御前」と詠まれた熊野権現の[[御子神]]である。この系統の神社には、[[藤白神社]]（和歌山県海南市）末社の楠神社（通称 子守社）などがある。
他にも、[[率川神社]]は子守明神と呼ばれている。
同名の神社として以下のものがある。 == 水分神系 == *[http://kamakurasi.air-nifty.com/blog/2024/05/post-70ad8e.html 子守神社] ([[神奈川県鎌倉市]]) *[[子守神社 (可児市)|子守神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]） *[[子守神社 (京丹波市)|子守神社]]（[[京都府]][[京丹波町]]） *[[籠守勝手神社]]（[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]） *[[子守勝手神社]]（京都府[[長岡京市]]） == その他 == * [[子守神社 (千葉市)]]（[[千葉県]][[千葉市]]） * [[子守神社 (河津町大鍋)]]（ねのかみじんじゃ、[[静岡県]][[河津町]]大鍋） * [[巨椋神社#子守神社|子守神社 (宇治市)]]（[[巨椋神社]]境内社）（[[京都府]][[宇治市]]） * [[率川神社]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]） * [[子守神社(平群町)|子守神社]]（[[奈良県]][[平群町]]）
== 関連項目 == * [[子安神社]] * [[子安地蔵]] ** [[子安地蔵堂]] ** [[子安地蔵寺]] * [[子守地蔵]] * [[子育地蔵]] * [[水天宮]]
<!-- {{曖昧さ回避}} --> {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりしんしや}} [[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:同名の神社]] [[Category:出産]] [[Category:産育]] [[Category:育児]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "同名の神社" to "Shrine disambiguations"
revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=}} - [[Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=}} in [[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[Nara, Nara|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=}} - [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=}} - [[Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=}}, [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]

They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]
