revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131925935}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|qq=|1=Koyasu-Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447885}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131925935}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|qq=|1=Koyasu-Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447885}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|qq=|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|qq=|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|qq=|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|qq=|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|qq=|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|qq=|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131925935}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|qq=|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|qq=|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]

=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131925935}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|1=Koyasu-Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447885}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131925935}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|1=Koyasu-Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447885}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131925935}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|1=Koyasu-Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447885}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子守神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447878]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.
Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
== Lineages == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is [[:en:Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]].
=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage === The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the [[:en:Ryōjin Hishō|Ryōjin Hishō]]: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in [[:en:Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan, Wakayama]].
Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], which is also known as Komori Myōjin.
== Shrines by Type == === Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura, Kanagawa]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - [[:en:Ichinomiya, Aichi|Ichinomiya, Aichi]] * {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]
=== Other Shrines === * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|ja|子守神社 (河津町大鍋)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kawazu, Ōnabe)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131925935}} - Located in Ōnabe, [[:en:Kawazu, Shizuoka|Kawazu, Shizuoka]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, [[:en:Uji|Uji, Kyoto]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} - [[Nara City, Nara|Nara City, Nara]] * {{ill|Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja|子守神社 (平群町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Heguri)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-dō|ja|子安地蔵堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-dō|1=Koyasu-Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447885}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū]]

'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.

Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].

== Lineages ==
=== Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage ===
Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara}}.

=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage ===
The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|en|Ryōjin Hishō|WD=Q3010584|lt=Ryōjin Hishō}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in {{ill|Kainan, Wakayama|en|Kainan, Wakayama|WD=Q841309|lt=Kainan, Wakayama}}.

Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

== Shrines by Type ==
=== Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage ===
* {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura, Kanagawa}}
* {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - {{ill|Kani, Gifu|en|Kani, Gifu|WD=Q853805|lt=Kani, Gifu}}
* {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja|子守神社 (京丹波町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyotanba)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Kyotanba, Kyoto|Kyotanba, Kyoto]]
* {{ill|Kagomori Katate Shrine|ja|籠守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagomori Katate Shrine|1=Komori Katte Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603933}} - {{ill|Ichinomiya, Aichi|en|Ichinomiya, Aichi|WD=Q270810|lt=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}
* {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]

=== Other Shrines ===
* {{ill|Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|ja|子守神社 (千葉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Chiba City)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447880}} - [[Chiba City, Chiba|Chiba City, Chiba]]

* {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|ja|巨椋神社#子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kyoto, Uji)|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}} - Located within {{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|巨椋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Shrine|1=Ogura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63148006}}, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji, Kyoto}}

The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|en|Ryōjin Hishō|WD=Q3010584|lt=Ryōjin Hishō}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in {{ill|Kainan, Wakayama|en|Kainan, Wakayama|WD=Q841309|lt=Kainan, Wakayama}}.

Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

'''Komori Shrine''' (子守神社, also read as Komamori Jinja) refers to various {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s across Japan. As the name suggests, these shrines enshrine the "Komori no Kami" (child-protecting deity) and serve as protectors of children. They may be enshrined as the primary deity ([[ujigami]]) or as subsidiary shrines within larger shrine precincts. The name can also be written as 籠守 or 子護.

Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].

== Lineages ==
=== Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage ===
Mikumari-no-Kami, originally a [[:en:Water deity|Water deity]] responsible for water distribution, came to be associated with child protection as the pronunciation of "Mikumari" (みくまり) shifted to "Mikomori" (御子守), meaning child protection. The head shrine of this lineage is {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara}}.

=== Kumano Hongū Taisha Lineage ===
The eighth shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha, known as Komori-no-Miya, enshrines a deity referred to in the {{ill|Ryōjin Hishō|en|Ryōjin Hishō|WD=Q3010584|lt=Ryōjin Hishō}}: "The children of the gods, such as the Wakamiya of Hachiman and the Wakōji and Komori Gozen of Kumano." Shrines in this lineage include the subsidiary {{ill|Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja|楠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusu Shrine (Komori Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090605}} in {{ill|Kainan, Wakayama|en|Kainan, Wakayama|WD=Q841309|lt=Kainan, Wakayama}}.

Additional associations include {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, which is also known as Komori Myōjin.

== Shrines by Type ==
=== Mikumari-no-Kami Lineage ===
* {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|子守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Komori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447878}} - {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura, Kanagawa}}
* {{ill|Komori Shrine (Kani)|ja|子守神社 (可児市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine (Kani)|1=Komamori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447881}} - {{ill|Kani, Gifu|en|Kani, Gifu|WD=Q853805|lt=Kani, Gifu}}

* {{ill|Komori Katate Shrine|ja|子守勝手神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Katate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 向日神社|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} - [[Nagaokakyo, Kyoto|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]

=== Other Shrines ===

Komori shrines are associated with two main lineages: the [[Mikumari|Mikumari-no-Kami]] lineage and the lineage of the eighth shrine of [[Kumano Hongū Taisha]].
